name: Whitney Holt
subject: FWD: Watch Abigail’s new TV ad
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Spanberger for Congress
body:
| [ ](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick)
---
| Peter, Just reaching out with a quick note to make sure you saw Bettina's email this morning about our first TV ad. I'm excited about the ad, because it really shows the heart of what Abigail is all about: [**working together, finding solutions, and supporting people.**](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/uVUpLQ1Nf_3n8oajX1x4qXEVVB4Av0HMekofeMxdVAk=?refcode=kick) Our campaign embodies these values. The people of Central Virginia are Abigail's first priority, and our campaign doesn't take any corporate PAC money, because Abigail wants voters to know she is focused on their interests, not special interests. **[But because we don 't take that money, our campaign is made possible through the generous support of our grassroots supporter network.](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/uVUpLQ1Nf_3n8oajX1x4qXEVVB4Av0HMekofeMxdVAk=?refcode=kick) [_Can you make a contribution today to support our work?_](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick) ** | | [CONTRIBUTE](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick)
---
Thank you,
_Whitney_

**\---------- Forwarded message ---------**
From: Bettina Weiss
Date: Tue, Jul 21, 2020 at 7:31 AM
Subject: Watch: Abigail's new TV ad

Dear Peter,
Our campaign released our first television ad today, and I wanted you to be the first to see it:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick)
This ad, titled "Called to Serve," focuses on Abigail's record of public service and her commitment to serving the people of Central Virginia.
We have one of the most competitive races in the country, and the winner could come down to just a handful of votes. **That 's why it's crucial we get Abigail's message to every last voter in VA-07. **
Abigail has served the people of Central Virginia with integrity and always puts the needs of her constituents and her focus on solving problems first. She works across party lines to get things done. I am proud of her work, and we need to spread her ad far and wide to let others know her impact, too.
_**[Can you make a contribution right now to help us show this ad to everyone across VA-07?](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick)**_
Our opponent and his allies will be spending millions against us on negative attack ads to try and drown out our message and cast doubt on Abigail's record of service. **Our victory in November is going to come down to whether Abigail 's hopeful message and record of focused, steady leadership is heard over the noise. **
This campaign is focused on the critical issues that matter to Virginians, like rising healthcare and prescription drug costs, the reopening concerns of small businesses, and the need for expanded opportunity for the next generation.
At the core of our campaign is the belief that meaningful conversations - not extreme partisan attacks - are how we bring people into our campaign and how we'll win this election. _**[If you believe this too, will you make a contribution to our campaign today?](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick)**_
| [CONTRIBUTE](https://act.myngp.com/el/SVD-sEdJXySW1VXNlXF92nkNlbUdDzsJr6NKg6WpQRE=/JsRKseI2r6P5-6douFL55avQ5I-MuI8Nd2xSoO2zVhw=?refcode=kick)
---
Thank you for your support,
_Bettina _

With Republicans playing dirty, we have to fight TWICE as hard and raise TWICE as many resources to win.

**- Rachel Maddow on voter suppression in Georgia -
"IT IS A WAKE-UP CALL FOR WHAT IS COMING IN NOVEMBER AND WHAT REPUBLICANS ARE GOING TO TRY TO DO."**
|
---
|
---
Team, Rachel Maddow is 100% right: The voter suppression in Georgia's primary election was not an accident - it was the latest example of Republicans trying to buy this election and cling to power.
The GOP will do _whatever it takes_ to win - including suppressing our right to vote - and there's simply too much at stake to let them get away with it:

**Right now polling shows Reverend Raphael Warnock (D) and unelected Sen. Kelly Loeffler (R) NECK AND NECK in Georgia's toss-up race - which _The Washington Post_ says is key to deciding Senate control!**
With Republicans playing dirty, we have to fight TWICE as hard and raise TWICE as many resources to win. Nate Silver's FiveThirtyEight says Democrats can flip this seat and take back the Senate, but we can't do it without grassroots support.
** We have a historic opportunity to elect voting rights champion Reverend Warnock as Georgia's first Black senator, win in Georgia and end Mitch McConnell's majority once and for all. name: Powered By People (BetoORourke.com)
subject: Can you join us this Thursday?
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BETO FOR AMERICA
body:
[1]Powered By People

We had our second phone bank since the runoff last night, contacting
voters all across the state. In just one night, we were able to make
304,939 phone calls!

It was an amazing start to our push to reach out to and identify voters
who will support Democrats in Texas. But there are a lot more voters to
contact, and we need an even bigger army of volunteers to help us stay on
track.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/event/voter-contact-phone-bank_attend/34385?source=ema20200721-98398&t=1001&akid=98398%2E444211%2Eo9z7KJ ]So, this Thursday night, Beto is calling and recruiting volunteers. Can
you join him and help grow our volunteer base for our critical future
efforts? RSVP right now to get all the details to join.

[ https://act.betoorourke.com/event/voter-contact-phone-bank_attend/34385?source=ema20200721-98398&t=1002&akid=98398%2E444211%2Eo9z7KJ ]Virtual Phone Bank with Beto
[ https://act.betoorourke.com/event/voter-contact-phone-bank_attend/34385?source=ema20200721-98398&t=1003&akid=98398%2E444211%2Eo9z7KJ ]Thursday, July 23, 6:30pm CT[ https://act.betoorourke.com/event/voter-contact-phone-bank_attend/34385?source=ema20200721-98398&t=1004&akid=98398%2E444211%2Eo9z7KJ ]Get more info or RSVP »

Remember what’s at stake: every single call we make is so that in
November, we can stop Trump forever and win a Democratic majority in the
State House. In an election this close, your support could be the tipping
point.

Hope to see you on the phone bank!

The Powered by People Team

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. Email is the most important way for us to reach you about opportunities to act. If you need to remove yourself from our email list, click here to unsubscribe: https://act.betoorourke.com/unsubscribe/unsubscribe/?t=1005&akid=98398%2E444211%2Eo9z7KJ

name: =?utf-8?Q?Future=20Generations?=
subject: Cori Bush Knocking the House Out of the Park!
body:
Progressive Insurgent Cori Bush A Future Generations Candidate! | | | | [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=ef525fb704&e=66979298f9)
---
| | |
## _CORI BUSH WILL WIN MISSOURI_
---
|
### **We're going all in for Future Generations candidate, Cori Bush!**
---
| [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=4ca2555438&e=66979298f9)
---
| Future Generations is proud to endorse Cori Bush for Missouri's 1st Congressional District. Cori, an educator and a nurse, has been a civil rights activist advocating for racial justice, police reform, healthcare, and environmental justice her entire life — not because it's popular, but because it's the right thing to do. We are down to the wire and need to help Cori win her primary election on Tuesday, August 4th!
---
| [VOLUNTEER FOR CORI!](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=df2b65cc33&e=66979298f9 "VOLUNTEER FOR CORI!")
---
|
---
|
> _"I’m honored to receive the endorsement of Future Generations PAC, because they know—like I know—that climate change is a crisis that we have to act swiftly and boldly to address if we want to leave our world habitable for the next generation. We need to fight with everything we’ve got to enact a Green New Deal, and that’s exactly what I plan to do in Congress.”
>
> — **Cori Bush** , Candidate for Congress (MO-01)_
---
|
---
|
#### **CORI HAS BUILT A NATIONWIDE & INTERNATIONAL COALITION**
---
| [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=5dcea2f41d&e=66979298f9)
---
From Ferguson to the current Black Lives Matter movement, Cori has been in the frontlines advocating for racial justice and police reform. Cori is ready to oust the old guard of representation from St. Louis. When our representatives are only doing for themselves, we have to find those who have always and will always fight for those who are voiceless, our most vulnerable. We have full confidence that Cori will be that person for Missouri's 1st Congressional District and the nation.
| [ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=83a9bf0cfd&e=66979298f9)
---
Cori inspired millions worldwide as they watched her journey in [Netflix's "Knock Down the House."](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=1dd34c8c6d&e=66979298f9) Together, Cori and Roza we're honored to travel to Argentina to build a coalition of people powered politics; meeting with Argentina's Congress, progressive militants, and new progressive candidates. We must do everything to ensure insurgent candidates who carry their communities in their hearts and minds, win and represent us in D.C.
|
---
| | Due to COVID-19 health and safety restrictions, we must focus on phone and text banking to get our candidates message to their constituents.**[ Will you invest today for seven generations into the future?](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=0e28ddf6c4&e=66979298f9)**
---
| [](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=6c0474856a&e=66979298f9)
---
| **_ON THE GROUND FOR CORI_**
If our success in Texas shows how effective we can be with just a few dollars. We want to repeat that smae success in Missouri. We will be hosting weekly canvassing, visibility, phone and text banking events for Cori. It will cost us around $2,800 to cover these costs. [Will you chip in to help us cover these expenses so we can keep winning?](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=046f21e164&e=66979298f9)
---
| [DONATE TO HELP COVER ACTION COSTS](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=3f133efc4e&e=66979298f9 "DONATE TO HELP COVER ACTION COSTS")
---
|
#### **A MESSAGE FROM ROZA CALDERON**
---
| Supporting Cori Bush was a no brainer. I was fortunate enough to be part of a small group of activists who were nominated by their communities to run for Congress in 2018 under Justice Democrats and Brand New Congress(BNC). A few of us attended a training in DC hosted by BNC and one by one went up to give our stump speech in front of those in attendance. When Cori went up, the energy changed. The power she carries is inspiring! I left that summit thinking, "We need Cori's in every state. In every race. Fighting for the people." That is my commitment.

Roza Calderon
Executive Director
[roza@futuregenerations.earth](mailto:roza@futuregenerations.earth)
---
[ ](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=8ccaa95617&e=66979298f9)
|
---
| | | | | | | | [](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=af97b04637&e=66979298f9)
---
| | [](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=3e3909ee7e&e=66979298f9)
---
| | [](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=198a6ed493&e=66979298f9)
---
| | [](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=035462a381&e=66979298f9)
---
|
---
| _Copyright © 2020 Future Generations PAC, All rights reserved._
You are receiving this email because you opted in via our website.

**Our mailing address is:**
Future Generations PACPO Box 1861Olympia, WA 98507-2394
[Add us to your address book](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/vcard?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=9c2d36475c)

Want to change how you receive these emails?
You can [update your preferences](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/profile?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=9c2d36475c&e=66979298f9) or [unsubscribe from this list](https://Earth.us19.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&id=9c2d36475c&e=66979298f9&c=0905b0e24e).

[](http://www.mailchimp.com/email-referral/?utm_source=freemium_newsletter&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=referral_marketing&aid=94f5440de46d3dc73892719b6&afl=1)

name: Team Amy
subject: twice as hard
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Amy Kennedy for Congress
body:
|
#
Hello, We have just 3 months left until Election Day, which means we have no time to waste right now. We are up against big money and powerful forces. Donald Trump’s fundraising machine is heavily rewarding our opponent turncoat Congressman Jeff Van Drew for switching parties and pledging his “undying loyalty” to Trump. This race is going to be extremely close and you can bet Jeff Van Drew is going to do everything he can to buy this election. name: Joe Biden
subject: It's time for historic investments in caregiving and early education, Peter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Here's how we'll do it: |
---
Peter --

Earlier today, I met with Sarah Johnson, and her 3-year-old son, Noah, at his preschool. She said the program does wonders, helping Noah learn his letters and numbers, socialize with other kids, and develop his personality.

But like so many parents, she's worried -- about her job as a dental assistant and noticing changes in Noah's behavior since he hasn't been in school since March.

She has had to lean on her parents to help. It's really tough. Peter, I bet you feel it too.

Even before the pandemic, millions of working families have faced enormous financial and personal strains trying to raise their kids and care for their parents or loved ones living with a disability.

It's the sandwich generation that includes everyone from an 18-year-old daughter caring for her mother who suddenly gets sick, to a 40-year-old Dad raising his child and caring for his own aging parents.

The joy and love are always there. But it's hard. It's really, really hard.

Families are squeezed emotionally and financially. They need help, but often can't find it or afford it.

Right now, we are trapped in a caregiving crisis within an economic crisis within a public health crisis. And President Trump has shown time and again he fails the most important test as President -- to care, for you and for all of us. To see people where they are, what they're going through, and where they want to be.

I understand. I've been a single parent. Even though I had more support than a lot people going through tough times, I know how hard it is. When Jill and I got married, I saw how difficult it was for her to resume her teaching career while being a new mom. We cared for our parents to the end, my mom moved in with us, and our entire family cared for our Beau when he died of cancer.

We know what so many of you are going through. While everything just feels different and we're just not sure if everything will be okay, I'm here to tell you that it can, and it will, be okay.

**That 's why today, as the next plank of my Build Back Better plan, I'm announcing a new plan for mobilizing a 21st century care and early education economy to deal with the caregiving crisis in America.**

[**In a moment I 'll explain the details, but first I have to humbly ask, Peter: can you make your first donation to power my campaign and build a new caring economy? **](https://go.joebiden.?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q32020?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=50&amounts=25,50,100,250&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac") _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

[Click here to express donate $250 >>](https://go.joebiden.?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q32020?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=250&amounts=25,50,100,250&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&express_lane=true")

[Click here to donate another amount >>](https://go.joebiden.?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q32020?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=50&amounts=25,50,100,250&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac")** Peter, this plan is about easing the squeeze on working families that are raising their kids and caring for aging loved ones. And it's about creating jobs with better pay and career pathways for caregivers, and showing them the dignity and respect they deserve.

Here's how we'll do it:

**First, let 's start with caring for aging relatives and loved ones with disabilities.** We'll expand access to home and community-based services and support for seniors and people with disabilities through Medicaid. And we'll help states hire 150,000 community health workers -- real heroes who go into communities and make sure folks are getting the care they need.

**Second, we can also make high-quality child care more affordable and accessible.** My early childhood education plan is straightforward: Every 3- and 4-year-old child will get access to free, high-quality preschool. Low- to middle-income families won't spend more than 7% of their income on high-quality child care for children under 5 years old. And the most hard-pressed working families won't have to spend a dime.

**Third, as we expand access to caregiving, we need to pay and support our caregivers.** They do God's work, but home health workers aren't paid much. They have few benefits. Forty-percent are still on SNAP or Medicaid. So my plan is direct: Give caregivers and early childhood educators a much needed raise and new career pathways, because no one should have to work more than one job to make ends meet. And I will make sure these workers have a choice to join a union and collectively bargain to protect their rights and earned benefits.

It's a triple-win.

**Peter, we can do this. The bottom line is that a mobilized caregiving and education workforce would save working families time and money, get their loved ones the care and education they need, and put more people to work.**

When we usually talk about a jobs package, there is a big push for shovel-ready jobs. But that's what care jobs are. These workers are ready now. These jobs can be filled now. Allowing millions of people, primarily women, to get back to work now.

[**That 's why I need you to help power this campaign, Peter. If you can, will you chip in your first $5 today? **](https://go.joebiden.%3D/?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "http://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q32020?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=5&amounts=25,50,100,250&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B2_BBB-caregiving_X__F1_S1_C1__ac") _If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:_

clean_body: Here's how we'll do it: | Peter -- Earlier today, I met with Sarah Johnson, and her 3-year-old son, Noah, at his preschool. She said the program does wonders, helping Noah learn his letters and numbers, socialize with other kids, and develop his personality. But like so many parents, she's worried -- about her job as a dental assistant and noticing changes in Noah's behavior since he hasn't been in school since March. She has had to lean on her parents to help. It's really tough. Peter, I bet you feel it too. Even before the pandemic, millions of working families have faced enormous financial and personal strains trying to raise their kids and care for their parents or loved ones living with a disability. It's the sandwich generation that includes everyone from an 18-year-old daughter caring for her mother who suddenly gets sick, to a 40-year-old Dad raising his child and caring for his own aging parents. The joy and love are always there. But it's hard. It's really, really hard. Families are squeezed emotionally and financially. They need help, but often can't find it or afford it. Right now, we are trapped in a caregiving crisis within an economic crisis within a public health crisis. And President Trump has shown time and again he fails the most important test as President -- to care, for you and for all of us. To see people where they are, what they're going through, and where they want to be. I understand. I've been a single parent. Even though I had more support than a lot people going through tough times, I know how hard it is. When Jill and I got married, I saw how difficult it was for her to resume her teaching career while being a new mom. We cared for our parents to the end, my mom moved in with us, and our entire family cared for our Beau when he died of cancer. We know what so many of you are going through. While everything just feels different and we're just not sure if everything will be okay, I'm here to tell you that it can, and it will, be okay. That 's why today, as the next plank of my Build Back Better plan, I'm announcing a new plan for mobilizing a 21st century care and early education economy to deal with the caregiving crisis in America. "" If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: "" "" "" "" "" "" Peter, this plan is about easing the squeeze on working families that are raising their kids and caring for aging loved ones. And it's about creating jobs with better pay and career pathways for caregivers, and showing them the dignity and respect they deserve. Here's how we'll do it: First, let 's start with caring for aging relatives and loved ones with disabilities. We'll expand access to home and community-based services and support for seniors and people with disabilities through Medicaid. And we'll help states hire 150,000 community health workers -- real heroes who go into communities and make sure folks are getting the care they need. Second, we can also make high-quality child care more affordable and accessible. My early childhood education plan is straightforward: Every 3- and 4-year-old child will get access to free, high-quality preschool. Low- to middle-income families won't spend more than 7% of their income on high-quality child care for children under 5 years old. And the most hard-pressed working families won't have to spend a dime. Third, as we expand access to caregiving, we need to pay and support our caregivers. They do God's work, but home health workers aren't paid much. They have few benefits. Forty-percent are still on SNAP or Medicaid. So my plan is direct: Give caregivers and early childhood educators a much needed raise and new career pathways, because no one should have to work more than one job to make ends meet. And I will make sure these workers have a choice to join a union and collectively bargain to protect their rights and earned benefits. It's a triple-win. Peter, we can do this. The bottom line is that a mobilized caregiving and education workforce would save working families time and money, get their loved ones the care and education they need, and put more people to work. When we usually talk about a jobs package, there is a big push for shovel-ready jobs. But that's what care jobs are. These workers are ready now. These jobs can be filled now. Allowing millions of people, primarily women, to get back to work now. "" If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: "" "" "" "" "" "" Thank you, Joe | | "" "" "" "" Has the pandemic affected you personally, or do you just want to take a break from emails for a couple weeks? We understand. "" Any donor history information in this email reflects what we have on file for this specific email address. If you have donated with a different email, with a check, or with a spouse -- thank you so much! We have that on file and cannot thank you enough for supporting this campaign.

subject: WATCH LIVE: President Trump holds news conference
body:
At 5 p.m. ET today, President Trump will hold a news conference from the White House Briefing Room. | | | | [ ](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=c831ee83cd&e=3ee4985eea)
---
| The White House • July 21, 2020
---
| | |
# **WATCH LIVE: President Trump holds news conference**

**At 5 p.m. ET today,** President Trump will hold a news conference from the White House Briefing Room.

[Click here to watch President Trump live at 5 p.m. ET](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=f7f6c06462&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## **Press Secretary: The violence in Portland must end**

**“The well-organized mob** in Portland has become increasingly aggressive, especially against law enforcement officers,” Press Secretary Kayleigh McEnany told reporters during a press briefing this morning.

**“Individuals have thrown bricks** , chunks of concrete, glass bottles, feces, balloons filled with paint, pigs’ feet, slingshots to hurl ball bearings, and batteries at Federal agents and the courthouse. Multiple attempts to barricade officers in the Hatfield Courthouse have occurred.” The violence has raged on for more than 50 days.

🎬 [**Press Secretary: Democrats continue to put politics above peace**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=695845beef&e=3ee4985eea)

**In response,** President Trump deployed federal officers to Portland to protect Federal Government property. He has also repeatedly offered support to state and city leaders to help quell the violence on their streets.

“**By any objective standard** , the violence, chaos, and anarchy in Portland is unacceptable,” McEnany said. “Yet, Democrats continue to put politics above peace, while this President seeks to restore law and order.”

**Portland isn’t alone** —in large cities across the country, local leaders are standing down in a failed attempt to placate violent rioters and mobs. Ten people were killed and 60 more wounded in shootings across Chicago this past weekend alone. In New York City, 205 shootings last month marked the most violent June for the city in 24 years.

**The pattern is clear**. When city leaders turn their backs on policing, our most vulnerable communities pay the price. “Stopping or discouraging the cops from disproportionately policing these neighborhoods isn’t a blow for justice. It’s an obstacle for upstanding, low-income citizens who are trying to lead decent lives,” Rich Lowry wrote on Sunday for the [_National Review_](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=7cff749d38&e=3ee4985eea).

**“People who live** in the affected communities know this and, to their credit, often say it. But their voices don’t get the megaphone of anti-police agitators.”

**President Trump** will not stand by as American citizens are terrorized by rioters or criminals in any of our cities. In Portland, Democrat leaders complained when the President sent in federal officers to protect federal property. But if mayors and governors refuse to do their jobs to restore peace and safety, President Trump will.

**“We’re not going** to let this happen in our country,” he told reporters yesterday in the Oval Office. “We’re going to have more federal law enforcement.”

[**President Trump: For 51 days, they were ripping down that city**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=0736cde056&e=3ee4985eea)

🎬 **Watch** : [**The police are restricted from doing anything**](https://whitehouse.us19.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4da4828d48b4efa4fc1138955&id=6ccdc5722e&e=3ee4985eea)
---
|
---
|
## Photo of the Day
---
|
---
| **President Trump signs a Presidential Memorandum to exclude illegal aliens from the apportionment base following the 2020 census** | July 21, 2020
---
|

---

name: Gina Raimondo
subject: Keeping the lights on
body:
Your RI COVID-19 News Update
---
| | | | | |
---
| Hello friend,
We're now three weeks into Phase 3 of our reopening plan, and overall things are going well. We're still seeing relatively low numbers of new cases, and much of our economy has reopened. But the economic impacts of this crisis remain completely devastating. Too many Rhode Islanders across the state are still struggling to make ends meet. In order to emerge from this crisis stronger than before, we need to provide relief. Today, I have some good news. The Rhode Island Public Utilities Commission voted last week to extend an order that electric and gas service cannot be shut off or sent to a collection agency.
---
| [ ](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0010Cqyuzyb4u-g2uXP8KGX8ZHsvHK8b9CJqNeyyqdyMug2ho_i1egSAUBU0i6rjj2Nv6NVhJDLZgn1_GxhqAQJ-RAVAA9QIz58u7_GVzi_wE3_fJaEqPJibbKoLGsi288POBLz0fKd42m0L-ReWuaVvhxAvw_EOpgq2zrPJEPrLuA560VLHDLwlQR1zV36_MIdYrNuD1wQx6s=&c=JRjujVYivaAI3Wh89w2xfyUdp4vJKhCUK7gMG5KNsaXeiaE44xDWiA==&ch=fYZoHJcaMrh9Ds1ehdqQR5hD2RyrkR8sLXRB6bhlzuXW90OONxyT8g==)
---
| Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's [COVID-19 data dashboard](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0010Cqyuzyb4u-g2uXP8KGX8ZHsvHK8b9CJqNeyyqdyMug2ho_i1egSAUBU0i6rjj2Nv6NVhJDLZgn1_GxhqAQJ-RAVAA9QIz58u7_GVzi_wE3_fJaEqPJibbKoLGsi288POBLz0fKd42m0L-ReWuaVvhxAvw_EOpgq2zrPJEPrLuA560VLHDLwlQR1zV36_MIdYrNuD1wQx6s=&c=JRjujVYivaAI3Wh89w2xfyUdp4vJKhCUK7gMG5KNsaXeiaE44xDWiA==&ch=fYZoHJcaMrh9Ds1ehdqQR5hD2RyrkR8sLXRB6bhlzuXW90OONxyT8g==).
---
| The PUC's order extends the moratorium on shutoffs for all National Grid residential customers until September 30, 2020. These two additional months will allow Rhode Islanders to get back on their feet without the threat of a shutoff looming overhead. To further help Rhode Islanders pay their bills when they are able to without facing penalties, the Commission has also directed utilities to suspend late fees, credit card charges, and interest until further ruling of the PUC. There's also no question that this crisis is the most devastating for those who can least afford it, and it's important that these folks get the assistance they need. The Public Utilities Commission order also extends the moratorium on service terminations for nonpayment for National Grid customers enrolled in the electric or gas low income rate through November 1, 2020. November 1 is also the start of the winter moratorium, meaning that this order protects certain low-income Rhode Islanders from service termination until April 15, 2021. I'm grateful to the PUC for taking these important steps, but our work to ensure that every Rhode Islander can get back on their feet is just getting started. Stay tuned.
We're all in this together,
-Gina Raimondo Governor
P.S.: Information is our greatest weapon in the fight against COVID-19, If you or someone you know wants to sign up to receive these emails, you can use [this link](http://r20.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0010Cqyuzyb4u-g2uXP8KGX8ZHsvHK8b9CJqNeyyqdyMug2ho_i1egSAViOmTtvkL3W9WmCTHyd9z3g8atdOaMkRzbnVp_C6itbcVTF9XqPUByzXtr1s9KWjXS0VFw4t5cIW8LO0NZMvgXTL5C7aD0yS0MKdc3XTZaCYE7GdMJAsYwccBe1NfLWMJM_Jqms1338NipkH0w9VNhajZJ2BLD_mJe_0iLiiDPG7cOhNocp9SIr_NasMeZzOx5qo_rd3jT03LvlxYlgW9XzM-b1Mf67pfmeky0YRNWOFQh0LsDsg1TgbPriMihs2WTevAZkNVVoGDi2_9mO7g0=&c=JRjujVYivaAI3Wh89w2xfyUdp4vJKhCUK7gMG5KNsaXeiaE44xDWiA==&ch=fYZoHJcaMrh9Ds1ehdqQR5hD2RyrkR8sLXRB6bhlzuXW90OONxyT8g==).

We're still seeing relatively low numbers of new cases, and much of our economy has reopened. But the economic impacts of this crisis remain completely devastating. Too many Rhode Islanders across the state are still struggling to make ends meet. In order to emerge from this crisis stronger than before, we need to provide relief. Today, I have some good news. The Rhode Island Public Utilities Commission voted last week to extend an order that electric and gas service cannot be shut off or sent to a collection agency. | == | Click the graph to see check the Department of Health's ==. | The PUC's order extends the moratorium on shutoffs for all National Grid residential customers until September 30, 2020. These two additional months will allow Rhode Islanders to get back on their feet without the threat of a shutoff looming overhead. To further help Rhode Islanders pay their bills when they are able to without facing penalties, the Commission has also directed utilities to suspend late fees, credit card charges, and interest until further ruling of the PUC. November 1 is also the start of the winter moratorium, meaning that this order protects certain low-income Rhode Islanders from service termination until April 15, 2021. I'm grateful to the PUC for taking these important steps, but our work to ensure that every Rhode Islander can get back on their feet is just getting started. Stay tuned. name: REPLY REQUESTED - Jon Ossoff for Senate
subject: 📣Jon wants to hear from you
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JON OSSOFF FOR SENATE
body:
Do you have a minute? |
---
Friend,

Jon Ossoff is surging in the polls, and Republicans are panicking about losing Georgia.

**Our momentum is thanks to grassroots supporters like you -- truly, you are the beating heart of this campaign.**

**That's why Jon wants to hear from you.**

**[Will you take a minute to complete our BRAND NEW Summer Grassroots Supporter Survey to let us know which issues are most important to you and your family?](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2156/5b2c1286/3dd1e6ea/25f1654a/345772653/VEsH/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://ossoff.bsd.net/j/step/summer-survey")**
[](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2156/5b2c1286/3dd1e6ea/25f1654a/345772653/VEsE/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://ossoff.bsd.net/j/step/summer-survey") | | [Respond now >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2156/5b2c1286/3dd1e6ea/25f1654a/345772653/VEsF/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://ossoff.bsd.net/j/step/summer-survey")
---
Amid a public health and economic crisis, we have an incompetent Trump Administration and a Senate controlled by self-dealing influence peddlers like Jon's opponent, David Perdue.

**It's never been clearer we need a new generation of leadership in Washington.**

**We need to hear directly from our grassroots supporters, especially since during this public health emergency we can't do live in-person events.**

So we've put together a new Summer Grassroots Supporter Survey for you, friend.

**[Please fill out our quick one-minute survey to let Jon know what matters most to you.](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2156/5b2c1286/3dd1e6ea/25f1654a/345772653/VEsC/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://ossoff.bsd.net/j/step/summer-survey")**

_Your answers will help guide our thinking as we work to elect Jon and deliver for the people._

| [Take our supporter survey >>](https://go.electjon.com/page/m/177e2156/5b2c1286/3dd1e6ea/25f1654a/345772653/VEsD/?g=_FNKN1DdY9BfCqelLAMjFg "https://ossoff.bsd.net/j/step/summer-survey")
---
Thank you for sharing your thoughts. We're grateful to have you on this team.

\- Team Ossoff

name: Christy Smith
subject: ICYMI: Trump trying to take away health care in the middle of a pandemic
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress
body:
| []()
---

| | | **Donald Trump's administration asked the Supreme Court to strike down the Affordable Care Act.**

**[If you think that this is the time to expand health care coverage -- not take it away from millions -- sign our petition telling the Senate to pass the House's ACA expansion plan now>> ](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba8f/29776869/9116fe0/569ef4b8/1371103316/VEsH/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MjFfYWNhcGV0Mi1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/s/2007_email_christy_ACA?source=em20_200721_acapet2-full")**
---

Peter --

In a middle-of-the-night filing to the Supreme Court, Donald Trump asked the nine justices to dismantle the Affordable Care Act -- and take health care away from millions of people.

He did this in the midst of a historic pandemic, as coronavirus case numbers spiked here in California and across the country.

He did this while folks are worried about the putting food on the table and paying rent due to layoffs and furloughs.

He did this knowing that 27 million Americans may have already lost health insurance because they've lost their jobs in the worst unemployment crisis since the Great Depression.

**The Trump administration's lawsuit risks stripping health care coverage from another 23 million of us when we need it most.**

**[While Trump was trying to take away health care coverage, Democrats in the House passed a bill to _expand_ the Affordable Care Act (Mike Garcia voted against it). Let's make sure it passes the Senate -- add your name if you support expanding health care coverage.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba8f/29776869/9116fe0/569ef4b8/1371103316/VEsE/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MjFfYWNhcGV0Mi1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/s/2007_email_christy_ACA?source=em20_200721_acapet2-full")**
[SIGN THE PETITION](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba8f/29776869/9116fe0/569ef4b8/1371103316/VEsF/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MjFfYWNhcGV0Mi1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/s/2007_email_christy_ACA?source=em20_200721_acapet2-full")
I believe that health care is a human right -- because I know just how difficult it can be when you can't afford the treatment you need.

**I saw my mom go through it just five years ago.**

My mom, a nurse who knew all too well the health risks of not managing her diabetes, couldn't afford her insulin. She spent decades taking care of others, including HIV patients during the early days of the AIDS crisis, but she couldn't afford to get the care _she needed_.

**She hid her rationing from my brothers and me while she waited for Medicare to kick in. She passed away at 67.**

**I believe that no one should get sick because they're poor and can't afford their treatment, or become poor because they're sick and our health care system bankrupts them.** And it makes no sense that, although we're one of the richest nations in the world, our people spend more on prescription drugs than in any other country.

We need our health care system to actually _work for people, not profits_ \-- especially during this public health crisis. We need to enhance and expand the ACA, not destroy the law that gave millions of folks access to health insurance for the first time.

**[Please, add your voice to tell the Senate that you support expanding the ACA. Together, we can make health care accessible to millions more.](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba8f/29776869/9116fe0/569ef4b8/1371103316/VEsC/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MjFfYWNhcGV0Mi1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/s/2007_email_christy_ACA?source=em20_200721_acapet2-full")**
[SIGN THE PETITION](https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/m/14f6ba8f/29776869/9116fe0/569ef4b8/1371103316/VEsD/p/eyJlM3R5WldaamIyUmxmWDA9IjoiZW0yMF8yMDA3MjFfYWNhcGV0Mi1mdWxsIn0%3D/?g=TRA5GMQot1BeTNifStrWt_A "https://secure.christyforcongress.org/page/s/2007_email_christy_ACA?source=em20_200721_acapet2-full")
Thank you,

Christy

clean_body: | []() | | | Donald Trump's administration asked the Supreme Court to strike down the Affordable Care Act. "" Peter -- In a middle-of-the-night filing to the Supreme Court, Donald Trump asked the nine justices to dismantle the Affordable Care Act -- and take health care away from millions of people. He did this in the midst of a historic pandemic, as coronavirus case numbers spiked here in California and across the country. He did this while folks are worried about the putting food on the table and paying rent due to layoffs and furloughs. He did this knowing that 27 million Americans may have already lost health insurance because they've lost their jobs in the worst unemployment crisis since the Great Depression. The Trump administration's lawsuit risks stripping health care coverage from another 23 million of us when we need it most. "" "" I believe that health care is a human right -- because I know just how difficult it can be when you can't afford the treatment you need. I saw my mom go through it just five years ago. My mom, a nurse who knew all too well the health risks of not managing her diabetes, couldn't afford her insulin. She spent decades taking care of others, including HIV patients during the early days of the AIDS crisis, but she couldn't afford to get the care she needed. She hid her rationing from my brothers and me while she waited for Medicare to kick in. She passed away at 67. I believe that no one should get sick because they're poor and can't afford their treatment, or become poor because they're sick and our health care system bankrupts them. And it makes no sense that, although we're one of the richest nations in the world, our people spend more on prescription drugs than in any other country. We need our health care system to actually work for people, not profits \-- especially during this public health crisis. We need to enhance and expand the ACA, not destroy the law that gave millions of folks access to health insurance for the first time. "" "" Thank you, Christy "" Christy Smith is a community leader from CA-25 who has fought for her neighbors in Ventura and LA county on the school board and in the California state legislature. Now she needs our help to defeat Mike Garcia and flip this top pick-up House seat blue in November. Help elect a champion for our health care, our schools, and our rights. "" | | Paid for by Christy Smith for Congress. ---|--- |

name: Joe Biden 2020
subject: Your donation today will help protect Obamacare
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Biden for President
body:
Every day that Donald Trump is in office, the Affordable Care Act is at risk. |
---
Peter --

Every day that Donald Trump is in office, the Affordable Care Act is at risk.

After he failed to repeal Obamacare, Trump has been doing everything he can to sabotage it. And he's actively trying to get the _entire law_ struck down in court -- including protections for people with pre-existing conditions.

Under a President Biden, we won't have to worry about threats to this historic law. Especially not during a pandemic. Instead, we'll build on the ACA to reduce health care costs and make it clear that health care is a right, not a privilege.

[**But we can only ensure that every single American has access to the care they need if we defeat Donald Trump in November. That 's why we're asking: can you chip in your first $5 to power this campaign today? **](https://go.joebiden.?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q32020?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B3_ACA_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=50&amounts=25,50,100,250&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B3_ACA_X__F1_S1_C1__ac") | If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

Peter, while the Trump administration keeps trying to rip health care away from millions, Joe Biden is focused on making quality, affordable health care available to every American.

He'll give Americans a new choice - a Medicare-like public option. He'll work to lower premiums and extend coverage to more working Americans. He'll expand coverage to low-income Americans. And more.

But right now under President Trump, we can't make any of that progress a reality. Instead, we have to spend each day hoping that the courts uphold Obamacare and trying to reduce the harm that Trump's sabotage is causing.

[**We can 't afford to keep going on like this, Peter. Will you help power the movement to protect and expand on the Affordable Care Act? Anything you're able to contribute today will make a real difference.**](https://go.joebiden.?g=m9NVbVNw52hcv-nFCZYEQPA "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/biden2020_fr_q32020?refcode=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B3_ACA_X__F1_S1_C1__ac&amount=50&amounts=25,50,100,250&email=dpwillis67%40gmail.com&firstname=Peter&lastname=Willis&zip=18326&city=Cresco&state=PA&phone=&addr1=%0A&utm_medium=email&utm_source=bsd&utm_campaign=JB_EM_FR_2020.07.21_B3_ACA_X__F1_S1_C1__ac")

name: Mark Kelly
subject: Can I have a minute to explain?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Maggie for NH
body:
Friends --
In just a moment, I am going to ask you to split a contribution to my campaign for Senate and Senator Maggie Hassan's campaign. Please give me a chance to explain why this request -- especially today -- is so important.
As we venture into the summer months, which are notoriously slow for fundraising, we have to continue growing our support and building up this campaign.
Mitch McConnell is hoping that our fundraising slows down. And what's concerning is that it actually did at this point last summer, so we really have to make sure we keep up the pace.
That is why I'm hoping that we can get 3,290 donations today, which is the number of donations we need every day to stay on track to hit our goal this month. So what do you say?
[**Will you split a $3 contribution today between mine and Senator Hassan 's campaigns to help us stay on track to hit our goal this month? If we can keep up the pace, especially during the slower summer months, we're going to win.**](https://act.maggiehassan.com/page/m/f7fc588/2a380d31/23756475/f0ada4/2369044773/VEsH/p/eyJlM3NnWVdKd1lXZGxJSDE5IjoiMjAyMF9td2g0a2VsbHkiLCJlM3NnY21WbVkyOWtaU0I5ZlE9PSI6ImUyMDIwMDcyMV9NV0g0TUtfbmEiLCJlM3NnVEZCQlFWRjFaWEo1VTNSeWFXNW5JSDE5IjoiMywyNSw1MCwxMDAsMjUwIn0%3D/?g=YHsLejpTVzRXNXNZdVQ0tHA "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/2020_mwh4kelly?refcode=e20200721_MWH4MK_na&amounts=3,25,50,100,250")

Please give me a chance to explain why this request -- especially today -- is so important. As we venture into the summer months, which are notoriously slow for fundraising, we have to continue growing our support and building up this campaign. Mitch McConnell is hoping that our fundraising slows down. And what's concerning is that it actually did at this point last summer, so we really have to make sure we keep up the pace. That is why I'm hoping that we can get 3,290 donations today, which is the number of donations we need every day to stay on track to hit our goal this month. So what do you say? |

Whether in Texas, or the midwest, or the west coast, who we have in this Senate seat will impact us all **[which is why we need your help to reach voters and defeat Sen. * **34% of Texas voters are still undecided ** about who they're going to support in November -- that's a lot of voters we can reach.
* Texas demographics have been changing, and now **we 're the biggest battleground state of 2020.**

Cornyn have ignored everyday Texans in favor of their corporate PAC donors, the gun lobby, Big Pharma, and countless other special interests. Texas is ready for a change, and with your help, we're going to win and kick out not just Sen. Cornyn, but also Mitch McConnell and all their big-money backers.

name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: Happy Birthday, CFPB!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Warren Democrats
body:
It was a David versus Goliath moment. Even when the banks spent hundreds of millions of lobbying dollars, we won. For once, American families beat back the big banks. | | | [](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285250f5/45c30650/1cc666f3e/66e875bc/3969444823/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2In0%3D/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://elizabethwarren.com?source=em-header&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")
---
Ten years ago today, President Obama signed the Dodd-Frank Act into law, creating the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. It had been a long, tough fight, in the wake of the financial crisis -- and it's important that we never forget what brought us to that fight.

Like so many of our nation's problems, the financial crisis was a toxic mixture of racism, corruption, and shameless greed. Predatory mortgage lenders started by targeting Black and Brown communities, where they began clawing away at the hard-earned wealth of Black and Brown families.

And too few people in power could be relied on to care. Not the investors who were making money hand over fist, not the regulators who were cozy with the banks, not the pundits who blamed the borrowers, not the lenders who were boosting their profits. They didn't care because it was only happening in certain communities -- communities of color. But it didn't stop there.

In the crash of 2008, millions of people lost their jobs, their homes, and their savings. And maybe one of the most galling parts was that the Wall Street megabanks whose recklessness helped crash the economy weren't ashamed. Nope. Just the opposite. They took taxpayer handouts, then they doubled down with armies of lobbyists and lawyers, trolling the halls of Capitol Hill spending a million dollars a day to bury Dodd-Frank and kill the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau before it was ever signed into law.

The pundits all thought we would lose that fight. After all, the big banks had all the money in the world. But we had the people. And we got organized.

Individuals who had never been in politics (including me) were suddenly knee deep in working on plans to get us out of the crisis. We were blessed to have great leadership in the White House, with President Obama standing firm even when some Democratic advisers were telling him to throw the agency overboard.

But President Obama didn't fight alone. Vice President Joe Biden fought alongside him. He worked at it, calling on his relationships in Congress to help get a workable bill and pull it across the finish line. And we did it. With his help and so many others, we made it through the Senate without a single vote to spare.

**It was a David versus Goliath moment. Even when the banks spent hundreds of millions of lobbying dollars, we won. For once, American families beat back the big banks.

But on the day we won, Republicans and bank lobbyists declared that it was only half-time. They said they would continue their war against the CFPB -- and their war against the American consumer. And they did. So you and I have to fight back.**

[Add your name to say that you'll keep fighting to protect the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau -- so it can be a strong watchdog for decades to come. ](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285250f5/45c30650/1cc666f3e/66e875bf/3969444823/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVQxNVBfU0dOX0RGS19FXzIwMjAwNzIxX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-dodd-frank?source=EMT15P_SGN_DFK_E_20200721_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

Legislation to eliminate the CFPB has been introduced in the House and the Senate in every single Congress since the agency was created in 2011. Republicans have engaged in one harebrained scheme after another to undermine the agency's leadership. And despite the agency's track-record of non-partisan protection of consumers, and despite the agency's popularity, Republicans have targeted the CFPB's budget and sought to chop back its enforcement authority.

Why have Republicans been so dead set against the CFPB? Why have they made repeal of the agency an act of faith? I think they are threatened by the CFPB. Deep-down threatened. And here's why.

Donald Trump and Republicans are scared of the CFPB because they are buddies with the Wall Street banksters that the CFPB stands up to.

Back in 2016, then-candidate Trump made big promises to drain the swamp, ignore the lobbyists, and stand up to Wall Street. He lied.

In fact, even before he set foot in the Oval Office, he filled his Transition Team and incoming Administration with lobbyists, special interests, and corporate insiders. He isn't going to drain the swamp. He lives in the swamp.

But there's another reason: The CFPB is a roadmap for structural change -- big, structural change.

Together, we bent the levers of power in Washington to benefit working families over the wealthy and well-connected. We demonstrated a model that can be used to rewrite the rules of power all across our broken systems, a model for change that would work from our economy to our democracy to our public safety and justice system.

Donald Trump and Republicans really can't stand Dodd-Frank and the CFPB because it shows that government can be a powerful force for good. Since its creation, the CFPB has delivered for American consumers. It has:
* Helped more than 26 million Americans directly
* Forced banks and financial institutions to return more than $12 billion directly to people they cheated
* Handled more than 2.2 million complaints
* Put in place commonsense protections for American service members and vets
* And, the part we can never quite measure, is that over the past decade, millions of people have not been cheated because some financial outfit with a sleazy idea looked around and realized there was a watchdog that would bite.

The creation of the CFPB teaches us that we can make government work, not just for the wealthy and well-connected, but for everyone.

But if Republicans get their way, shred the CFPB, and let Wall Street write its own rules, it will be another gut punch for working families.

[We've got to fight to protect the CFPB the same way we fought to create it: side by side. If you agree that we need an agency looking out for consumers and protecting people from financial scams, add your name.](https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/m/285250f5/45c30650/1cc666f3e/66e875bf/3969444823/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZsYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVm1hWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVjZhWEFsSlE9PSI6IjE4MzI2IiwiZTNzZ2NtVm1ZMjlrWlNCOWZRPT0iOiJFTVQxNVBfU0dOX0RGS19FXzIwMjAwNzIxX1YxUzFfUCJ9/?g=4qwscq_HJ6FcsC0VbCsFfMA "https://act.elizabethwarren.com/page/s/ew-dodd-frank?source=EMT15P_SGN_DFK_E_20200721_V1S1_P&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=18326")

Thanks for being a part of this. And happy 10th birthday, CFPB!

Elizabeth

clean_body: It was a David versus Goliath moment. Even when the banks spent hundreds of millions of lobbying dollars, we won. For once, American families beat back the big banks. | | | "" Ten years ago today, President Obama signed the Dodd-Frank Act into law, creating the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau. It had been a long, tough fight, in the wake of the financial crisis -- and it's important that we never forget what brought us to that fight. Like so many of our nation's problems, the financial crisis was a toxic mixture of racism, corruption, and shameless greed. Predatory mortgage lenders started by targeting Black and Brown communities, where they began clawing away at the hard-earned wealth of Black and Brown families. And too few people in power could be relied on to care. Not the investors who were making money hand over fist, not the regulators who were cozy with the banks, not the pundits who blamed the borrowers, not the lenders who were boosting their profits. They didn't care because it was only happening in certain communities -- communities of color. But it didn't stop there. In the crash of 2008, millions of people lost their jobs, their homes, and their savings. And maybe one of the most galling parts was that the Wall Street megabanks whose recklessness helped crash the economy weren't ashamed. Nope. Just the opposite. They took taxpayer handouts, then they doubled down with armies of lobbyists and lawyers, trolling the halls of Capitol Hill spending a million dollars a day to bury Dodd-Frank and kill the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau before it was ever signed into law. The pundits all thought we would lose that fight. After all, the big banks had all the money in the world. But we had the people. And we got organized. Individuals who had never been in politics (including me) were suddenly knee deep in working on plans to get us out of the crisis. We were blessed to have great leadership in the White House, with President Obama standing firm even when some Democratic advisers were telling him to throw the agency overboard. But President Obama didn't fight alone. Vice President Joe Biden fought alongside him. He worked at it, calling on his relationships in Congress to help get a workable bill and pull it across the finish line. And we did it. With his help and so many others, we made it through the Senate without a single vote to spare. It was a David versus Goliath moment. Even when the banks spent hundreds of millions of lobbying dollars, we won. For once, American families beat back the big banks. But on the day we won, Republicans and bank lobbyists declared that it was only half-time. They said they would continue their war against the CFPB -- and their war against the American consumer. And they did. So you and I have to fight back. "" Legislation to eliminate the CFPB has been introduced in the House and the Senate in every single Congress since the agency was created in 2011. Republicans have engaged in one harebrained scheme after another to undermine the agency's leadership. And despite the agency's track-record of non-partisan protection of consumers, and despite the agency's popularity, Republicans have targeted the CFPB's budget and sought to chop back its enforcement authority. Why have Republicans been so dead set against the CFPB? Why have they made repeal of the agency an act of faith? I think they are threatened by the CFPB. Deep-down threatened. And here's why. Donald Trump and Republicans are scared of the CFPB because they are buddies with the Wall Street banksters that the CFPB stands up to. Back in 2016, then-candidate Trump made big promises to drain the swamp, ignore the lobbyists, and stand up to Wall Street. He lied. In fact, even before he set foot in the Oval Office, he filled his Transition Team and incoming Administration with lobbyists, special interests, and corporate insiders. He isn't going to drain the swamp. He lives in the swamp. But there's another reason: The CFPB is a roadmap for structural change -- big, structural change. Together, we bent the levers of power in Washington to benefit working families over the wealthy and well-connected. We demonstrated a model that can be used to rewrite the rules of power all across our broken systems, a model for change that would work from our economy to our democracy to our public safety and justice system. Donald Trump and Republicans really can't stand Dodd-Frank and the CFPB because it shows that government can be a powerful force for good. Since its creation, the CFPB has delivered for American consumers. It has: Helped more than 26 million Americans directly Forced banks and financial institutions to return more than $12 billion directly to people they cheated Handled more than 2.2 million complaints Put in place commonsense protections for American service members and vets * And, the part we can never quite measure, is that over the past decade, millions of people have not been cheated because some financial outfit with a sleazy idea looked around and realized there was a watchdog that would bite. The creation of the CFPB teaches us that we can make government work, not just for the wealthy and well-connected, but for everyone. But if Republicans get their way, shred the CFPB, and let Wall Street write its own rules, it will be another gut punch for working families. "" Thanks for being a part of this. And happy 10th birthday, CFPB!
